short_description: Shopping, culture, and world-class art in KC's most iconic neighborhood.
long_description:
The Country Club Plaza and surrounding Midtown arts district offer one of Kansas City's most walkable and rewarding urban experiences — a full day of world-class art, green space, and great dining all within a few blocks of each other. 

Start at the Nelson-Atkins Museum of Art, one of the country's premier art institutions with a permanent collection spanning 5,000 years and a sculpture garden that's worth the visit on its own. Plan to arrive hungry — Rozelle Court, the museum's elegant café set inside a stunning atrium, makes for a memorable lunch before or after you explore the galleries. Free admission means there's no reason to rush. 

From there, make your way to the Kemper Museum of Contemporary Art just a few blocks away, where rotating exhibitions and a strong permanent collection of modern and contemporary works offer a sharp contrast to the Nelson's breadth. Also free. 

Next, take a quiet detour through Kauffman Legacy Park, a beautifully landscaped reflection space on the Kauffman Foundation campus — a good reset between museum stops. Nature lovers should also pencil in the Anita B. Gorman Conservation Discovery Center, a free Missouri Department of Conservation facility with native plant gardens, aquariums, and wildlife exhibits. 

Cap the day at the Country Club Plaza — KC's iconic Spanish-inspired outdoor district with fountains, distinctive architecture, and some of the city's best dining and shopping. It's especially beautiful in the evening.
